item.title: resolveProviderAlias() omits Hetzner — clawhq deploy create --provider hetzner silently rejected
item.intent: Every provider with a working adapter must be usable from the CLI. Hetzner is a first-class provider — users choosing sovereignty on European infrastructure must not hit a broken CLI gate.
item.description: FEAT-003 implemented the Hetzner Cloud provider adapter (src/cloud/provisioning/providers/hetzner.ts) and CloudProvider type includes 'hetzner'. However, the resolveProviderAlias() function in src/cli/index.ts (line 3528-3537) only maps 'digitalocean', 'do', 'aws', 'gcp' — no 'hetzner' or 'hz' alias. Consequence: clawhq deploy create --provider hetzner exits with 'Invalid provider' error even though the adapter exists and works. Two error messages (lines 3378, 3520) also show the wrong provider list, omitting Hetzner. Fix: add 'hetzner' and 'hz' to the aliases map; update both error messages to include hetzner (hz).
steps: Add hetzner and hz entries to resolveProviderAlias() aliases map in src/cli/index.ts
Update error message at line 3378 to include hetzner (hz) in provider list
Update error message at line 3520 to include hetzner (hz) in provider list
Verify clawhq deploy create --provider hetzner proceeds past alias resolution
Verify clawhq deploy credentials --provider hz stores credentials correctly
acceptanceCriteria: clawhq deploy create --provider hetzner does not exit with Invalid provider error
clawhq deploy create --provider hz resolves to hetzner provider
Both deploy create and deploy credentials error messages list all four providers including hetzner (hz)
No other provider aliases are broken by the change
